tldr: Headlines divide sharply between two dominant threads: a record-breaking Russian drone-and-missile barrage on Kyiv, and the arrest of Zelenskyy's former chief of staff on money-laundering charges; peace prospects are absent from the headline layer, economic strain is just beginning to register, and EU sanctions framing provides the only external-pressure counter-narrative.

evidence.quote: Russia unleashes 1,500 attack drones on Ukraine, shattering prospects for peace
Russia Unleashes Heaviest Wartime Drone Assault On Ukraine, Hits Kyiv
Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1
Putin launches deadly assault with over 700 drones and missiles
Kyiv Court Orders Former Zelenskyy Adviser Yermak Jailed On $3 Million Bail
Yermak arrestato per corruzione a Kiev
Zelensky's former chief of staff named as suspect in major money-laundering probe
Ex-Zelenskyy aide tied to multimillion-dollar laundering probe

frames.sub_frame: No peace roadmap; Russian domestic politics

evidence.quote: Kremlin says no clear roadmap to end Ukraine war despite Putin's words
Putin Replaces Belgorod and Bryansk Governors
Foreign Minister Summons Russian Ambassador Over Brutal Transcarpathia Drone Assault
Stubb discusses frontline situation with Zelensky, heads to Vilnius
Diplomat: EU sanctions against Russia are working
EU sanctions Russians over 'systematic unlawful deportation' of children
Russian 'Shadow Fleet' Suspicions Linger in Dropped Albania Smuggling Case
Ukraine's Business Recovery Index Turns Negative for First Time Since 2023
As Russian Losses Mount, Signing Bonuses for Recruits Are Skyrocketing

isolation_top.note: Article is about US economic resilience to the Iran war — off-topic corpus artefact from multi-story pipeline overlap, not Ukraine coverage
Vatican News leads with a Ukrainian mother's grief narrative — personal-loss register semantically distant from military-strike and corruption corpus
Shadow fleet / Albania smuggling case headline is shipping-logistics framing — adjacent to sanctions story but semantically distant from kinetic-war corpus
Moscow Times and Meduza file entirely on Belgorod/Bryansk governor replacements — domestic political story that sidesteps international missile-barrage coverage
Combines NATO sanctions defense with an essay on 'dying for Bornholm' — strategic-analysis register distinct from kinetic-war corpus
Finnish NATO tech accelerator and Stubb-Zelensky meeting headlines — capacity-building and diplomatic framing distinct from barrage-and-corruption corpus

silences.what_they_covered_instead: China is the most important diplomatic actor whose position on a Ukraine settlement matters; it is entirely absent from this corpus
France provides major military support to Ukraine and shapes European alliance policy; its perspective is absent despite the barrage and ongoing talks
Iran's ongoing drone supply to Russia is a defining feature of the war's military balance; it appears only in the iran_opposition bucket and not through any state-media framing

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to include the specific drone count (1,500) and explicitly link the barrage to 'shattering prospects for peace' — connects military escalation to diplomatic consequences in a single headline
Only outlet leading with a first-person grief narrative from a Ukrainian mother — the sole headline treating the human-loss dimension as the primary angle
Only outlet tracking how a drone strike near the DPRK embassy affects Kim Jong Un's potential Moscow visit — a diplomatic-signal angle absent from all other headlines
Only outlet running a headline on the Ukrainian Business Recovery Index turning negative — the only headline-layer signal that the war economy is beginning to contract

bottom_line: Ukraine war headlines bifurcate between record aerial bombardment and a major corruption arrest in Kyiv, leaving peace diplomacy, economic recovery, and the Iran-drone supply chain entirely below the headline horizon.
